data.title: HIVE Digital Technologies Ltd. CEO plans to focus on expanding HPC business in 2026, aiming for $225 million revenue.
data.description: The CEO of HIVE Digital Technologies Ltd. recently announced plans to prioritize expanding the company's high-performance computing (HPC) business in 2026. In the third quarter earnings call, it was revealed that in 2025, the firm successfully scaled Bitcoin mining operations to 25 exahashes per second globally and launched a 300-megawatt facility in Paraguay. The focus for 2026 will be on increasing HPC's annual recurring revenue from $20 million to $225 million. To achieve this goal, HIVE intends to enhance its cloud by increasing graphics processing units from 5,000 to 11,000 units.

data.title: PureHealth Research introduces new vein supplements containing Butcher's Broom for circulation health.
data.description: On February 18, 2026, in Manassas, Virginia, PureHealth Research announced the expansion of its vein supplements line by introducing new formulations that include Butcher's Broom. This Mediterranean plant, known for its traditional circulation benefits, contains ruscogenins, active compounds that support vein health. The company is capitalizing on this centuries-old remedy to enhance their products and cater to individuals looking to improve circulation. This move showcases PureHealth Research's commitment to utilizing natural ingredients in their supplements to address specific health concerns.

data.title: Battery Ventures raises $3.25B to support tech companies globally, with 15 exit events in 2025 and $10B liquidity in 5 years.
data.description: Recently, Battery Ventures, a leading global technology investment firm, completed a new fund totaling $3.25 billion to support technology companies worldwide, particularly in the U.S., Europe, and Israel. This latest fund, Battery Ventures XV, follows 15 successful exit events for the firm in 2025. Over the past five years, Battery funds have generated over $10 billion in liquidity, showcasing the firm's diversified investment strategy across various stages. This development highlights the firm's commitment to backing innovative tech companies on a global scale.

data.title: Wolters Kluwer expands with legal AI workspace in Italy, part of pan-European growth with launches in 4 countries.
data.description: Wolters Kluwer Legal & Regulatory has introduced the Libra by Wolters Kluwer legal AI workspace ("Libra") in Italy. This launch combines Libra's AI technology with Wolters Kluwer's legal content. The rollout in Italy represents a milestone in Libra's pan-European expansion, which has included successful launches in the Netherlands, Poland, and Germany. The integration of Wolters Kluwer's German legal content further solidifies Libra's presence in Europe.
